_doc: Collects first_name via ask_open (branch on outcome), then determines lifestyle via ask_options (branch on outcome). Sets lifestyle='outdoor'|'indoor' for later variable branching.

moves.text: Hi there! I am your personal companion for today.
I would love to get to know you a little better.
Before we get started, what should I call you?

name_missing.text: No problem at all, we can continue without a name.

moves.text: Here is a quick question to start: would you say you are more of an outdoor or an indoor person?

outdoor_lifestyle.text: A fresh air enthusiast — I like that!

indoor_lifestyle.text: A cosy indoor kind of person — sounds comfortable!

_doc: Branches on the persisted 'lifestyle' variable before any ask move. Each branch path contains a 5-way ask_options followed by a 5-way branch on outcome — demonstrating two levels of branching driven entirely by Dialogflow intent recognition.

moves.text: Let's talk about what you enjoy doing in your free time.

outdoor.text: Which outdoor activity do you enjoy most: hiking, cycling, swimming, or gardening?

hobby_hiking.text: Hiking is wonderful! There is nothing quite like exploring a trail at your own pace.
The changing scenery and fresh air really do clear the mind.

hobby_cycling.text: Cycling is great — you can cover so much ground and it is a real workout.
Road cycling and trail cycling both have their own kind of thrill.

hobby_swimming.text: Swimming is such a full-body workout, and being in the water is so calming.
Open water or a pool — both have their own magic.

hobby_gardening.text: Gardening connects you to nature in such a hands-on, rewarding way.
Watching things grow that you planted yourself is genuinely satisfying.

hobby_other_outdoor.text: Whatever it is, spending time outdoors is always time well spent.

indoor.text: Which indoor activity do you enjoy most: reading, gaming, cooking, or crafts?

hobby_reading.text: Reading is wonderful — you can travel anywhere without leaving your chair.
Fiction or non-fiction, both open up entirely new worlds.

hobby_gaming.text: Gaming sharpens problem-solving and reflexes in a very engaging way.
The storytelling in modern games can be absolutely incredible.

hobby_cooking.text: Cooking is such a creative and practical skill all at once.
And you get to eat the results, which is always a bonus.

hobby_crafts.text: Crafting is so satisfying — there is real pride in something made with your own hands.
And every finished piece is completely yours.

hobby_other_indoor.text: Whatever keeps you busy indoors, I am sure it brings you a lot of joy.

_doc: Demonstrates three levels of nesting: ask_yesno → 3-way branch on outcome (yes case contains ask_open → 2-way branch on outcome), followed by a separate branch on the 'lifestyle' variable. Requires 'hobby' from the previous chitchat dialog.

moves.text: So you enjoy %hobby% — that is great to know.
Would you say %hobby% helps you relax and unwind?

hobby_relaxes_yes.text: That is wonderful. Hobbies that help you decompress are genuinely valuable.
When do you usually find time for %hobby%?

time_shared.text: Making that time for yourself is so important — keep protecting it.

time_missing.text: Finding a regular slot for it can make a real difference to how you feel.

hobby_relaxes_no.text: Interesting! Some hobbies are energising rather than relaxing.
It is great that %hobby% gives you that kind of boost.

hobby_relaxes_dontknow.text: That is a thoughtful question to sit with.
Pay attention next time you do %hobby% and notice how you feel afterwards.

moves.text: I find it interesting how a person's lifestyle shapes the hobbies they choose.

outdoor.text: Outdoor hobbies often attract people who crave space, movement, and that sense of freedom.
There is something deeply grounding about being out in the world.

indoor.text: Indoor hobbies often attract people who value focus, creativity, and their own rhythm.
There is real depth in activities you can pursue anywhere, any time.

_doc: Final narrative in the hobbies thread. Uses ask_open with llm_followup to generate a personalised encouraging response, then branches on whether a goal was shared.

moves.text: Thinking about everything we have discussed, I have one last question for you.
Is there something new you would like to try or explore — whether related to %hobby% or something completely different?
moves.llm_followup: Reply in one warm, encouraging sentence that echoes what the user just shared and motivates them to take a first small step. Sound genuine and human.

goal_shared.text: I will keep that in mind for our next conversation.

goal_missing.text: That is okay — sometimes the next thing finds you when you are not looking.

_doc: Personalised farewell using %first_name% and %hobby% substitution, both reliably set by the time this dialog runs.

moves.text: It was truly wonderful getting to know you, %first_name%.
I hope you get to enjoy plenty of %hobby% in the days ahead.
